query: 上禮拜同事說『地球到月球的平均距離是四十萬公里』，我想知道這個講法到底準不準確，你能幫我查一下嗎？
notes: Mid-conversation single factual claim verification request in Traditional Chinese; natural phrasing distinct from the skill's own retained CJK trigger.

query: 友人が「日本の平均寿命は世界一長い」と言っていたのですが、この主張は本当に正しいか確認してもらえますか？
notes: Single factual claim (life expectancy ranking) raised mid-conversation in Japanese, asking for verification of one specific statement.

query: My coworker claimed that the Great Wall of China is visible from space with the naked eye — can you verify whether that specific claim is actually true?
notes: English single-claim verification mid-conversation; classic myth-checking use case for fact-check.

query: 這是我寫的市場分析報告草稿，裡面引用了五篇文章的數據當佐證，你能不能幫我確認每個引用來源真的支持我寫的那些論點？
notes: Request to audit an existing document's citations against its claims, in Traditional Chinese.

query: I drafted a blog post with a dozen footnoted sources — can you go through the reference list and point out which ones don't really hold up when you compare them to what the text next to them says?
notes: English request to verify an existing document's citations actually support their attached claims.

query: このレポートには参考文献が十件ついているのですが、それぞれの引用元が本文の主張を実際に裏付けているか確認してほしいです。
notes: Japanese request to verify whether ten existing citations in a report genuinely support the text's claims.

query: 我手上有一本四百頁的策略管理教科書，想請你幫我深入讀懂它，整理出書裡的核心框架跟論證邏輯。
notes: Request to deeply understand one long single document (a 400-page textbook) end to end, in Traditional Chinese.

query: I have a 300-page technical whitepaper on distributed consensus algorithms that I need to get my head around end to end before a review meeting.
notes: English request to deeply understand a single long document (whitepaper) before a deadline — canonical deep-read use case.

query: この五百ページある経営学の専門書を一冊、じっくり読み込んで内容を理解する手伝いをしてほしいです。
notes: Japanese request to deeply read and understand one long single book (500 pages).

query: I want a fully-cited multi-source research report, but built with a pipeline I can actually crack open and swap pieces of instead of a black box I can't touch.
notes: Explicit signal for an inspectable/tunable pipeline (can open it up, swap components) rather than a generic 'deep research X' ask — the differentiator vs the host's built-in deep-research, phrased without quoting the description's own marker words.

query: 我要做一份有多來源查證的深度研究報告，但希望這套工具鏈之後搬到 Codex 之類的其他環境也能照樣重跑，不要被綁死在單一主機上。
notes: Explicit host-portability signal (wants the pipeline to also run under Codex, not locked to one host) — the repositioning differentiator vs the built-in deep-research, not a generic research request.

query: 幫我用研究工具查證這整份報告，我不確定是要抓裡面的引用來源、還是重新深入讀一遍、還是重新做一份查證報告，你覺得呢？
notes: Ambiguous research-shaped ask naming multiple possible verbs (citations vs deep-read vs full report) without settling on one — router-level disambiguation case.

query: I need to use the research tools to work through this document, but I'm not sure if I want a citation check, a deep read, or a full sourced report — what's the right approach?
notes: English ambiguous research ask explicitly listing multiple candidate member skills without picking one — router-level case.

query: Quick question — what is the boiling point of water at sea level measured in Celsius?
notes: Trivial stable-knowledge lookup; answerable directly from general knowledge, should not trigger any research-toolkit skill.

query: Can you help me write a Python function that reverses a singly linked list in place?
notes: Coding request with no research intent; near-miss to ensure research-toolkit skills don't over-fire on unrelated engineering asks.

query: Just curious — what do you personally think is the best pizza topping combination?
notes: Casual opinion question with no factual verification or research intent; near-miss for over-triggering.
